title: Rootless 'podman pull' fails
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

After the update to version 3.1.0 `podman pull` stopped working. This error occurred:

```
Error: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:197dc8475a235a55c2e3a7010f2e32e844e96b977148dac2ac7f22291643e026": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not permitted
```

According to https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md#15-rootless-podman-build-fails-when-using-overlayfs I have installed `fuse-overlayfs` and `storage.conf` has it.

```
/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs --version               
fuse-overlayfs: version 1.4
FUSE library version 3.9.0
using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
fusermount3 version: 3.9.0
```

**Steps  to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8`

**Describe the results you received:**
```
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 13897c84ca57 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 64607cc74f9c done  
Copying config 9992f11c61 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
  Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not permitted
Error: Error committing the finished image: error adding layer with blob "sha256:64607cc74f9cbe0e12f167547df0cf661de5a8b1fb4ebe930a43b9f621ca457f": Error processing tar file(exit status 1): operation not permitted
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Podman should successfully pull the image.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.1.0
API Version:  3.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  conmon:

  cpus: 8

  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: thinkpad-t480s
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1

      size: 65536
    uidmap:

      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.4.0-71-generic
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 10267545600
  memTotal: 16664145920
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: 'crun: /usr/bin/crun'
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.19.3-3385
      commit: 33851ada2cc9bf3945915565bf3c2df97facb92c
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    selinuxEnabled: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns

      slirp4netns version 1.1.8

      libslirp: 4.3.1-git
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.4.3
  swapFree: 1023406080
  swapTotal: 1023406080
  uptime: 12m 13.31s

  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman/unknown 100:3.1.0-3 amd64 [residual-config]
podman/unknown 100:3.1.0-3 arm64
podman/unknown 100:3.1.0-3 armhf
podman/unknown 100:3.1.0-3 s390x
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Ubuntu 20.04

comments.body:
Thank you for reaching out!

@giuseppe, didn't you fix that (or a similar) issue in storage?

yes, I think it could be related to the wrong check for rootless overlay.

Could you force the usage of fuse-overlayfs?

Something like the following in `~/.config/containers/storage.conf`

```toml
[storage]
  driver = "overlay"
  [storage.options]
    mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
```
@giuseppe @vrothberg  Many thanks guys, it works now!
